title: Automation edits
excerpt: Hello, it would be nice the option to be able to modify an automation when “edit automation” is selected, being able to take out some of the actions from the automation recipe or add new ones, without the need to do all …

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, it would be nice the option to be able to modify an automation when “edit automation” is selected, being able to take out some of the actions from the automation recipe or add new ones, without the need to do all over a new automation from the beginning to include the new modifications.

For example if I have an automation that when a status changes, somebody is assigned… and later on I see that also would be nice add to the same automation to set the date of assignment to today, that the new action could be added editing the original recipe, instead of adding a separated automation or redoing the original over again to include the additional action.

posts.author: KB11 (Kristina Burgess)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes!! If when you select edit automation you can have the same options to add or delete as you do when you originally created the automation it would be very helpful. I have to re make the automation when I forget something. While creating an automation is pretty painless, it still is burdensome to have to try and rebuild exactly what I built before with one minor tweak.

posts.author: ramsinator (William Ramsey)
posts.cookedHtml: I completely agree. This is one feature that would make this a lot more friendly. The one workaround that I wanted to share, if you click the 3-dots on the automation, you can select duplicate, and it will let you modify as if it were new. That is less painful than creating from scratch. Hopefully the Edit option gets added soon.

posts.author: thebrochproject (Robin Herrick)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. This is very puzzling for a new user.

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree. Editing an automation is very limited, it only lets you change the column / value selections but you cannot restructure the automation at all. It’s been very frequent that I have to rebuild the automation as a new creation, then delete the old one, just to add a condition or add one 1 more action.
Ideally the edit screen should look exactly like the create screen, where you can add and remove lines.

posts.author: longlive289s (Michael)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be so helpful. I currently in the middle of testing monday to see if it would be right for us and I’m pretty tired of recreated automations over and over and over

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Small tip: when you duplicate a custom automation you can edit it from the start (restructuring it etc). Save it and delete the one you wanted to modify.

Easy as that

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks ramsinator and basdebruin, for sharing the tip about the duplication. It seems as a helpful workaround while monday.com team will develop/improve the automation recipes edition.

posts.author: Tazz (Felix Pape)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi

It would be really nice if I could completely edit existing custom automations.
For example, add another “then” like I could on creation.

I really hope I don’t missed it completely, and this already is a possibility.

posts.author: Marc1 (Marc Rosser)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tazz. I’ll +1 on this.

We can’t add more conditions or actions to an existing custom automation without deleting it and rebuilding from scratch. Talk about effiency…

posts.author: ljohnson (Laura Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed - it doesn’t make sense to not be able to edit.

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t know how seriously Monday is taking this. But from my perspective as an administrator, there is no bigger barrier to my ability to move quickly to ensure user satisfaction and buy-in. Work OS needs a proper editor, otherwise we will end up with stagnation.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@psperry

It is technically correct that you cannot currently edit custom automations. Here is a workaround that is almost as good: Quick Tip - The Trick to Editing Custom Automations - YouTube

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
Watch Our Latest Video: Creating subitems using Make/Integromat The Monday Man (EASY) Way
Contact me directly here: Contact – The Monday Man

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, I worked that out and it does help in some cases. But you can’t change a trigger or insert an action, which often has me starting completely over. Come to think of it, if they would enhance the current “maker” to give better visibility of what you are doing, allow for moving things around, and changing the trigger, your method would be entirely acceptable. Come to think of it, I’ve had to start over several times because I can’t see what I’ve created so far or I realize I missed step three and I’m now on step twenty-one and there’s no way to go back and insert. Maybe I’m asking for the wrong thing.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@psperry

I wouldn’t say you’re asking for the wrong thing… monday has been quiet lately about new features… I’m looking forward to see what’s coming. Maybe this is part of it.

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: All good. I buckled down today and rewrote four automations with more than 25 actions each. These rolled up what I’ve learned the last few weeks so hopefully won’t have to touch them again soon. Now watch them release a awesome editor next week

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: By the way, you seem to be a leader here so i want to ask: Given the sheer number of users Monday enjoys, I’m surprised this community isn’t a LOT more active than it is. Am I missing something?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@psperry

No idea. My perception is that most people come to get an answer, get it (or not) and don’t come back until they have another question.

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: Understandable, but I feel I get more from a platform when I work to contribute as well as get the help I need. I’m a bit lopsided now at the beginning but I hope to even that out as I learn. Monday is a awesome product with huge potential. It would be great fun to make a contribution in that direction.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@psperry

That’s why I’m still here. I too believe in paying forward.

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: Love your YouTube channel btw. Was fortunate to find it early. It’s been a lot of help.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

We are really sorry for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here:
Automation edition as we plan to close this thread so we have we have all the votes in one, and can better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

Thanks!

posts.author: abarrero (Alfredo Barrero)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello team,

I would like to be able to edit my automations and add a second action to it. Once I click “add automations to board” I’m not able to add a second action.

For example:

Step 1 . Create Automation 1: When a Button is triggered change Status A to Done
Step 2. Add Automation to Board
Step 3. Edit Automation. When a Button is triggered change Status A to Done and notify People column
As far as I’m aware this third step can’t be done. The only workaround I found is create the automation from scratch.

Thank you

posts.author: abarrero (Alfredo Barrero)
posts.cookedHtml: Also I think Dashboards can get data from Boards with Mirror columns

posts.author: GeoffAirey (Geoff Airey)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this please (I’ve voted).

I’ve just created an automation with 7 actions and have then realised after saving that if I wish to add another action or change an action I have to recreate from scratch, which is tedious to say the least.

posts.author: GeoffAirey (Geoff Airey)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve just noticed that if you duplicate an Automation, it allows you to add additional actions, so this can do the job.

posts.author: GeoffAirey (Geoff Airey)
posts.cookedHtml: Don’t forget to disable or delete your old action or you may end up with actions running from both automations

posts.author: Hyperjase (Jason Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please - really frustrating when you’ve created quite a complex automation then can’t add to it.

posts.author: Franklin (Franklin Bains)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my +1 to this. It is a chore to duplicate/add/delete old, duplicate/add/delete old when you have more than one automation for similar functions that arise from slightly different stipulations.

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: You have no Idea how happy this makes me

posts.author: brenTRON (Brenton Wiberg)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 vote! This is so frustrating that you can fully edit an automation. It makes such a mess to have to create a new automation when you just want to add one more action to a trigger that already exists! It makes keeping your automations tidy very difficult!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

Excited to share that this feature has been released! *When editing a custom automation, you now have the option to fully adjust it by adding or removing conditions, triggers, and actions.

posts.author: rriker (Rusty Riker)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m still having an issue with this. I have a custom automation based on a status change. In the initial setup, there’s a prompt to select “to something” or “from something to something”. Once you’ve selected the type of trigger, it can’t be changed. You mentioned that triggers should be editable, but from what I’m seeing only the column and condition of the trigger is editable.

I previously created an automation with multiple actions when a status changes TO and, now that our workflow has changed a bit, I need to edit the trigger to be when a status changes FROM. I thought I’d try deleting the trigger as well thinking it would allow me to select a new one, but all the actions are deleted.

Is there something I’m missing, or should I put this in the features request? It would be great if the TO/FROM selection remained editable like the dropdown condition limiter.

posts.author: SERUAPK (SERUAPK)
posts.cookedHtml: Wink is a very prominent and natural auto-editing app.

posts.author: SERUAPK (SERUAPK)
posts.cookedHtml: If you want automatic video editing, Wink should be your top choice.
